input_statement: TASK ACCOUNTING IN THE BUG TRACKER
At any given time, there must be at least one task assigned to you in the "In Progress" status.
C: to at least one, at any given time, in the 'In Progress' status

input_statement: If you are working on a project alone, you create tasks and keep track of them yourself.
C: yourself, If you are working on a project alone

input_statement: For a project in development, this means you need to present the work plan in the form of tasks,
C: For a project in development, in the form of tasks

input_statement: break down a large task into subtasks, give time estimates for those subtasks.
B: a large task into subtasks,time estimates for those subtasks

input_statement: For a maintenance project, these are ongoing routine tasks.
Otherwise, the tasks are created by your technical manager.
Give the task a runtime estimate when you create it.
Change the status of the task when you take the task to work, send it for testing or review.
B: the status of the task, it for testing or review
C: when you take the task to work

input_statement: A task should have an executor!
The "Testing" and "Review" statuses are assigned to a task only if it is relevant for this project.
B: The "Testing" and "Review" statuses to a task
C: only if it is relevant for this project.

input_statement: IN WORK -> TESTING When a task is transferred to testing, the tester is set as the executor.
C: When a task is transferred to testing,

input_statement: TESTING -> IN WORK When a task is returned for revision, the developer is the executor.
C: When a task is returned for revision

input_statement: TESTING -> DONE When a task is transferred to the "ready" status, the task is assigned to the project manager.
C: When a task is transferred to the "ready" status

input_statement: For testing tasks, there should always be a primary task:
- for development (something the developer has been working on and now it needs to be tested)
or a regular daily test (daily/regularly tested builds).
In the first case, the developer transfers the task to the tester, the task is closed after acceptance.
C: In the first case, the task is closed after acceptance

input_statement: In the second case, the task can be open forever (with assignment to a tester), time and notes are logged into it.
C: In the second case, forever (with assignment to a tester), time and notes are logged into it.

input_statement: Mark the progress of the task.
Note the time spent daily.
If you need to attach a file (log, screenshot, etc.)
, you physically attach the file to the task.
Do NOT provide links to file sharing sites.
Keep working notes in the comments of the task.
C: in the comments of the task.

input_statement: If there is a difficult problem on the way to the goal,
or you've found a solution to a problem,
or if you have a question - write a comment.
Don't forget to give links to the source of the solution.
B: to give links to the source of the solution
